philippines.title: Lapu-Lapu barangay councilor arrested in Mandaue drug bust
philippines.description:
LAPU-LAPU CITY, Cebu — A 43-year-old barangay councilor from Lapu-Lapu City was apprehended after authorities allegedly found illegal drugs in his possession during a checkpoint on Thursday evening, June 25, 2026, along A.C. Cortes Avenue in Barangay Cambaro, Mandaue City. The suspect is a resident of Barangay Poblacion, Lapu-Lapu City. He is an incumbent barangay councilor and chairperson of the barangay’s Peace and Order Committee. Authorities allegedly recovered two small sachets of suspected shabu from the suspect’s pocket. READ: Mandaue City raid: 3 arrested with 18 grams of shabu in drug den Personnel from the Opao Police Station were conducting […]...
Keep on reading: Lapu-Lapu barangay councilor arrested in Mandaue drug bust

philippines.title: First Metro raises PSEi target to 6,700 on easing global risks
philippines.description:
MANILA, Philippines – First Metro Securities and DBS Bank raised their year-end PSEi target to 6,700 from 6,500 as easing geopolitical tensions improved market sentiment. In a joint report, the brokerages raised their target on expectations of modest earnings growth and a partial valuation recovery as US-Iran tensions de-escalate. READ: Iran-US talks to continue through the night The firms said the Washington-Tehran memorandum to halt hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz shifted market sentiment from crisis management to a gradual repricing of macroeconomic risks. While execution risks remain, they said the agreement offers a more constructive backdrop than investors […]...
Keep on reading: First Metro raises PSEi target to 6,700 on easing global risks

philippines.title: Sara Duterte defense panel files comment on prosecution's manifestation
philippines.description:
MANILA, Philippines — The defense panel of Vice President Sara Duterte’s impeachment trial on Friday filed its comment to the manifestation of the House prosecution team, including the request to unseal the box of the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) which contains her tax records.  This was confirmed by Atty. Michael Poa, Duterte’s defense lawyer, as Senate Secretary and Impeachment Clerk of Court Renato Bantug gave the panel until Friday to submit its comment electronically.  READ: House prosecutors: Open box of VP’s tax records “The Defense confirms that we have filed our Comment to the Prosecution’s Manifestation/Motion before the Impeachment […]...
Keep on reading: Sara Duterte defense panel files comment on prosecution's manifestation

energy.title: Crude Prices Sink as Traders Bet on More Iranian Oil
energy.description: August WTI crude oil futures kept falling sharply during the week ending June 26. Traders continued to take out the extra price they had added earlier because they worried about possible supply problems in the Middle East. The contract moved between a high of $78.14 and a low of $68.90 before finishing at $71.53. That was down $3.99, or 5.28 percent, from the week before. The selling went on for most of the week. Traders stopped focusing as much on the chance of oil supplies getting cut off in the Persian Gulf. Instead, they paid more attention…

energy.title: China’s Crude Imports Set to Hit Weakest Level Since 2016
energy.description: Chinese crude oil imports this month are on course to book an even weaker month than May, according to Kpler data, which sees the daily average at just 6.4 million barrels, as cited by Bloomberg. Data from Vortexa suggests a similar daily import level, the publication added. This would be the weakest import rate since October 2016, Bloomberg noted, and an 8% decline on May volumes. Those averaged 7.82 million barrels, according to customs data, down by 29% on the year and 17% on April. The May average was also a 38% drop on February volumes, before…
